title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Guided Contract Studio
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an exhaustive asynchronous state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

workshop case — Model idle → loading → success|failure for request 5. Each state owns only valid fields, stale request 4 cannot overwrite the result, and adding "cancelled" must create an exhaustiveness diagnostic until handled.
